body_txt: Societal values and mores will, regardless of what one tries to do to stop it, change and march on. There will always be those that stand up to fight in order to bring about positive change: suffragettes , slavery abolitionists , union organizers, gender and civil rights advocates , climate protesters , etc. Depending on the society and means available, those people will have different means of doing it: marches, protests, strikes , boycotts, civil disobedience. But technology also advances and changes , and with those changes come along new forms of protest. With the dawn of the 21st century, computation and informational technologies and access to the Internet became widespread . Virtually every household, office, whether belonging to a company or a government, factory and school has access to a computer, needed in order to store information and function. But, as with everything in human history, those can be (and often are) misused: corrupt corporate executives can fudge the numbers of their businesses and hide secrets of sketchy dealings, corrupt politicians keep evidence of less than savory affairs, corrupt bureaucrats can use the capabilities afforded by computers to manipulate the system for their own benefit… you get the picture. But what happens when someone decides to use those very same technologies against them? You get the Hacktivist. While more traditional activists are hardly Hopeless with Tech , Hacktivists are technologically savvy as they often rely on hacking computer mainframes and databases in order to acquire the material they required for their efforts, usually proof of wrongdoing to be released to the public or to the proper authorities, since after all, Information Wants to Be Free , although bringing IT infrastructure down to bring a problem to light is also an option. Hollywood Hacking may occur in more technologically advanced settings whenever they do their job, but they tend to be played as using as more true-to-life hacking methods. They, however, tend to not being as good at it as The Cracker , and rely more on Social Engineering tricks in order get to their targets. There tends to be some overlap with the Playful Hacker , especially if they fall into what is called " White Hat hacking ", which is accessing IT systems with the point of discovering security flaws to correct through ethical methods. Unlike other types of activists though, such as the Soapbox Sadie , their positions in the morality scale tend to lean towards the grey, as their methods can certainly cause a lot of problems to society, even if they are performed with well-meant intentions behind . Sometimes they can even enter the black and turn out to be a Not-So-Well-Intentioned Extremist . It is also very unlikely for them to be played for comedy , again due to the ethical and moral quandaries of hacktivism. Depending on the way the trope is played they can also fall under the Hacker Collective , usually as a Expy of Anonymous and WikiLeaks , while individual ones may instead be based on Edward Snowden or Julian Assange. Not to be confused with the British band Hacktivist . Owing to the ethical and moral concerns with hacking and hacktivism, No Real Life Examples, Please!
